title: Using monetary policy to affect the economy
description: Practice what you have learned about how central banks can use monetary policy to affect the economy in this exercise.
topic_path: Using monetary policy to affect the economy

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the effect of actions taken by the Central Bank of Hamsterville.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following is a reason that a central bank would take actions that lead to the change shown in this graph?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph showing a money market. Supply has increased causing a decrease in interest rates.

choices.content: It is concerned about high unemployment.
It was concerned about high inflation.
It was concerned that aggregate demand is too high.
It was concerned that the CPI was increasing too rapidly.
It wanted to engage in expansionary fiscal policy.

assessment_items.question_content:
The central bank of Ile conducted open market operations that changed the money market as shown in this graph.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What open market operation did they conduct, and what problem would they be trying to correct?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph of the money market showing an increase in the money supply leading to a decrease in the nominal interest rate.

choices.content: Buy bonds to fix a recession.
Buy bonds to fix high inflation.
Sell bonds to increase aggregate demand.
Sell bonds to lower the CPI.
Buy bonds to engage in expansionary fiscal policy.

assessment_items.question_content:
The nation of Vizzini has experienced the aggregate demand shock shown in this graph.

[[☃ image 1]]

**If a central bank wanted to use monetary policy to correct this situation, which of the following would they use?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph of the aggregate demand-aggregate supply model that shows an economy initially in long-run equilibrium. There is an increase in aggregate demand, leading to an increase in output beyond full-employment output and an increase in the price level.

assessment_items.question_content:
The central bank of Hamsterville conducted an open market sale of existing treasury bonds.

**Which of the following best describes a reason that the bank would do this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: raise interest rates, lower aggregate demand, and lower the price level.
lower interest rates, increase aggregate demand, and increase inflation.
lower interest rates, decrease aggregate demand, and lower inflation.
raise interest rates, increase aggregate demand, and lower inflation
increase interest rates and lower the unemployment rate

assessment_items.question_content:
The Central Bank of Montoya took a monetary policy action that resulted in the shift in aggregate demand indicated in the graph shown here.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following is a monetary policy action that the central bank most likely took to cause the change shown?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph of the aggregate demand-aggregate supply model that shows an economy initially producing output that is higher than full-employment output. There is a decrease in aggregate demand, which decreases output, returning it to full-employment output and decreasing the price level.

assessment_items.question_content:
The central bank implemented monetary policy that resulted in the shift shown in this graph:

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following graphs illustrates the effect of this change on the Aggregate Demand-Aggregate Supply model?**
[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A graph of the money market showing an increase in the money supply leading to a decrease in the nominal interest rate.

choices.content: ![A graph with a horizontal axis labeled "r GDP" representing real Gross Domestic Product and a vertical axis labeled "P L" representing price levels. Two distinct horizontal dashed lines indicating price levels P L one and P L two are present. There are two downward sloping lines labeled "A D" and "A D two". The line labeled "A D" intersects the vertical axis at a point marked "Y one" and "Y f," while the second line labeled "A D two" starts above the first line and slopes downwards, intersecting the vertical axis at "Y two." The graph includes a vertical dashed line extending from "Y f" to intersect the horizontal price levels and indicates equilibrium. The overall layout shows a typical aggregate demand and price level relationship.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23764cd032a45e66a36aaa6f4150827f5aa46e68)

![A two-dimensional graph with a vertical axis labeled "Price Level" and a horizontal axis labeled "real GDP." There are two upward-sloping curves, one labeled "AD" representing aggregate demand and the other representing two price levels labeled "P L 1" and "P L 2." The first price level intersects the aggregate demand curve at a point on the horizontal axis marked "Y f," while the second price level intersects at another point labeled "Y 2." A dashed vertical line extends from the points on the curves to the horizontal axis. Additionally, a filled black circle marks the intersection points on the horizontal axis at "Y 1" and "Y 2." The dimensions of the graph are specified as 346 by 345.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23e6bd8b23b1dd2f18d3bf90d3b5ae3286254447)

![A graph featuring two upward-sloping curves labeled "P L one" and "P L two," intersecting with two downward-sloping curves labeled "A D" and "A D two." The horizontal axis is labeled "r GDP," while the vertical axis is unlabeled. The intersection points of the curves are indicated by solid black dots at coordinates corresponding to "Y one," "Y two," and "Y f." Dashed lines extend horizontally from the intersection points to the vertical axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ae3c12c1003e166b816181f953054db52b305c73)

![A graph with two intersecting supply and demand curves on a Cartesian plane; the horizontal axis is labeled "real GDP," and the vertical axis includes price levels indicated by "PL1" and "PL2"; one upward-sloping curve is labeled "SRAS2," while the downward-sloping curve is labeled "AD"; the graph also features three points marked on the horizontal axis, labeled "Y1," "Yf," and "Y2," with dotted lines extending vertically from "Y1" and "Y2" to the price level axis, indicating equilibrium points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f359530310d966c49fb7526ef6d3d0d3752cf60)

![A graph with a vertical axis labeled "Price Level" and a horizontal axis labeled "Real Gross Domestic Product" displays two upward-sloping curves representing Aggregate Supply (SRAS) and Aggregate Demand (AD), with an additional downward-sloping Aggregate Demand curve (AD two). Two vertical lines labeled "Long Run Aggregate Supply one" and "Long Run Aggregate Supply two" intersect with the curves. Price Level one is marked with a dashed horizontal line crossing through the intersection point of the SRAS and AD curves, indicating equilibrium points labeled "Y one" and "Y two" along the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9996aa6798f7ef751fff49507566183ccd6f9f9e)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the short-run effect of a decrease in the money supply on output and the price level?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Output doesn’t change, price level decreases.

assessment_items.question_content:
An increase in real GDP has changed the demand for money, and the central bank wants to use open market operations to counter the effect of the change in demand on the nominal interest rate using open market operations.

**Which of the following is an open market operation that would have the desired effect on the nominal interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Economic conditions in Fredland have caused the demand for money to increase which has changed the nominal interest rate.

**If the central bank wants to counteract this change, which of the following is an appropriate open market operation to achieve that?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A banking innovation has made it easier for people in Hamsterville to convert their assets into money. As a result, the demand for money has decreased, and this has changed the nominal interest rate.

**If the central bank wants to counteract this change, which of the following is an appropriate open market operation to achieve that?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The central bank of Johnsrudia sold existing government securities in an open market operation. 

**Which of the following changes is the most likely result of this action?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The supply of loanable funds increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would most likely limit the ability of monetary policy to quickly correct an output gap?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It takes time to recognize that a gap exists.
People keep all cash in banks.
Banks decide not to keep any excess reserves.
People only consume a fraction of additional income.

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that an economy has an upward-sloping short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve.

**Which of the following would occur as a result of contractionary monetary policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Assume that an economy has an upward-sloping short-run aggregate supply curve. 

**Which of the following would most likely occur as a result of expansionary monetary policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The central bank of a country has implemented a policy of paying Interest on Reserves (IOR). The country's banking system has ample reserves. 

**Which of the following is most likely to occur if the central bank increases the IOR?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Banks will reduce their lending activities, leading to a decrease in money supply and potentially slowing economic growth.
Banks will increase their lending activities, leading to an increase in money supply and potentially stimulating economic growth.
The inflation rate in the country will automatically increase due to increased interest earnings for banks.
The amount of required reserves held by banks will decrease, leading to an increase in the money supply.
Banks will reduce their holdings of excess reserves, leading to an increase in the federal funds rate.

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that the central bank of a country decreases the Interest on Reserves (IOR).

** Given that the banking system of this country has ample reserves, which of the following outcomes is most likely?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Banks will increase their lending activities, leading to an increase in money supply and potentially stimulating economic growth.
Banks will reduce their lending activities, leading to a decrease in money supply and potentially slowing economic growth.
The inflation rate in the country will automatically decrease due to decreased interest earnings for banks.
The amount of required reserves held by banks will increase, leading to a decrease in the money supply.
Banks will increase their holdings of excess reserves, leading to a decrease in the federal funds rate.

assessment_items.question_content:
**In a banking system with ample reserves, which of the following is a primary consequence of a *decrease* in the Interest on Reserves (IOR) set by the Federal Reserve?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Banks may choose to lend more, leading to an expansionary effect on the economy.
Banks may choose to hold more reserves, thus decreasing the money supply.
Banks may choose to invest more in riskier assets, potentially increasing financial instability.
Banks may choose to decrease their lending activities, leading to a contractionary effect on the economy.
Banks may choose to diversify their portfolio, moving away from traditional lending activities.
